meta.title: India's Most Hilarious & Surprising Laws
meta.descriptionHtml: These are 100% real laws — just presented entertainingly. Disclaimer: CivicSense is for awareness & entertainment only, not legal advice.

items.title: Walking on the Wrong Side of the Road
items.description: Under the Motor Vehicles Act, pedestrians must walk on the right side of the road (facing traffic). Walking on the left can technically land you a fine.
items.funny: 😂 Half of Delhi would be in jail if this was enforced.

items.title: Spitting in Public is ILLEGAL
items.description: The Prevention of Spitting Act exists in many states. Spitting on streets, buses, railway platforms can invite fines up to ₹200–₹500 depending on the city.
items.funny: 😅 But Paan-wallahs seem to be unaware of this law.

items.title: Honking Near Hospitals is Illegal
items.description: Section 294 IPC prohibits acts that cause public annoyance. Honking near hospitals, schools and courts can result in fines under pollution control norms.
items.funny: 🤣 Indian roads didn't get this memo apparently.

items.title: Cutting a Tree Without Permission = Crime
items.description: Under the Indian Forest Act and Tree Preservation Acts of various cities, cutting even a single tree without municipal approval can invite heavy fines or imprisonment.
items.funny: 🌿 Your neighbour who cut that neem tree owes everyone an apology.

items.title: Littering in Public Carries Fines
items.description: Swachh Bharat Mission empowers municipalities to fine individuals ₹500–₹5,000 for throwing garbage, food wrappers, or any waste on streets and public spaces.
items.funny: 😬 That biscuit wrapper you threw? That's ₹500 going down the drain.

items.title: Smoking in Public Places – ₹200 Fine
items.description: COTPA 2003 prohibits smoking in public places including offices, restaurants, bus stops, educational institutions, healthcare facilities and more.
items.funny: 🚭 Even a bus stop counts as a public place under this law!

items.title: Wasting Electricity Can Be Penalised
items.description: Energy Conservation Act 2001 allows authorities to penalise excessive energy wastage in commercial premises. Many state electricity boards have their own rules too.
items.funny: 💡 That tubelight you leave on 24x7 is technically a civic crime.

items.title: Abandoning Your Pet on the Street is Illegal
items.description: Under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act, abandoning a domestic animal on streets can invite fines and even imprisonment up to 3 months.
items.funny: 🐕 That abandoned dog on your street deserves justice!

items.title: Wrong Side Parking Blocks Ambulances — and it's Illegal
items.description: Blocking fire hydrants, emergency lanes, or parking in no-parking zones invites heavy fines. In case of emergency, vehicles can be towed at owner's expense.
items.funny: 🚑 Your 5-minute parking may cost someone their life (and you ₹2000).
